client_explanation: SwarmJelly is the specialist AI we built to repair other AI. It's 4 billion parameters · about 1/100th the size of the big commercial models · but it's trained on 125,000 specific examples of what goes wrong with AI agents and how to fix them. You don't have to use it · the weights are open · you can host it yourself on a HoneyBox. We just ask that the receipts keep flowing through the network so the defense gets stronger for everyone.
cre_operator_meaning: In CRE every shop has a **specialist appraiser** for the asset class. The industrial broker doesn't call the residential appraiser. The cold-storage broker doesn't call the multifamily comp guy. You call the specialist · because the specialist has seen the comp set · knows the failure modes · knows what the building should pencil at. SwarmJelly is the specialist appraiser for **failed agent outputs**. It's seen 125,000 of them in training. It knows the failure shape on sight.

deed_receipt_impact.five_proofs_touched_raw:
- **Receipt fields touched**: `model_tag` · `parameter_count` · `temperature` · `endpoint` · `weights_checksum`
- **DDEED class impact**: every DDEED-DOV-REPAIR cites the SwarmJelly tag in the Proof of Origin
- **Books and records layer**: L1 PostgreSQL (model registry) · L4 Hedera HCS (weights checksum anchor) · L5 ENS (`swarmjelly-4b.models.defendable.eth`)
- **5 Proofs touched**: ORIGIN (which model produced the output) · QUALITY (the 7-day lift distribution backing the verdict) · TRUST (the open-weights checksum verifiable by anyone)

defendableos_definition: SwarmJelly is a 4B-parameter language model fine-tuned in-house on a 125,000-pair corpus organized as a **7-failure-mode × 5-RJ-task taxonomy** with a 25% per-mode distribution cap. It ships as `swarmjelly-4b:v1.2` (current production tag) and runs at `whale:11434`. It is the model that backs SwarmFixer · every refinery 5-task output is a SwarmJelly inference call at TEMP=0.05. The model is open-weights · MIT-with-receipt-clause license · operators can self-host on a HoneyBox.
defendableos_notes:
- SwarmJelly is the FIRST DefendableOS model with the receipt-clause license · the model itself is anchored
- The 7-mode × 5-task taxonomy was the breakthrough · prior in-house refiners didn't constrain the corpus shape · they overfit to whatever dominated
- The 25% cap is non-negotiable · v1.0 of this experiment overfit to HALLUCINATION (43% of corpus) · the cap kills that failure mode
- The TEMP 0.05 discovery is what made the model production-grade · at 0.7 it was useless (0.7% Honey) · at 0.05 it ships

🐝 *The specialist appraiser of failed agent outputs. Open weights. Receipted. The Hive verifies before i
evidence_required: Model tag in every refinery receipt
example:
> **Cook**: SwarmJelly-4B v1.2 cooked on the 125,000-pair corpus over 14 days on 4x RTX 6000 Blackwell at the operator-owned DC.
>
> **Corpus shape**: 7 failure modes × 5 RJ tasks = 35 cells · each cell ~3,500 pairs · no cell exceeded 4,500 pairs (25% cap held)
>
> **Production deployment**: `whale:11434` · model tag `swarmjelly-4b:v1.2` · TEMP locked at 0.05 in runtime config
>
> **Production rate (90-day window)**: 93.9% Honey rate · 3% Propolis rate · mean Repair Lift +0.18 · p10 +0.07 · p90 +0.31
>
> **Version history**: v1.0 (60.2% Honey · deprecated) · v1.1 (87% Honey · deprecated after v1.2 canary lift) · v1.2 (current)
>
> **Receipt anchor**: weights checksum `sha256:8c4e...d3a1` anchored on Hedera HCS topic 0.0.10291838 · ENS pointer at `swarmjelly-4b.models.defendable.eth`
failure_modes_raw:
| Mode | Description | Tribunal class on fail |
|---|---|---|
| `model_tag_drift` | Receipt cites a SwarmJelly tag not on the approved list | PROPOLIS · reject receipt |
| `corpus_imbalance` | Training corpus exceeds the 25% per-mode cap | JELLY · model quarantine |
| `endpoint_unreachable` | `whale:11434` doesn't respond · refinery halts | n/a · operational alert |
| `weights_mismatch` | Deployed weights don't match the public release hash | PROPOLIS · security incident |
| `silent_temp_override` | Inference runs at TEMP ≠ 0.05 without explicit doctrine waiver | PROPOLIS |
| `version_regression` | New version ships with worse aggregate lift than prior | n/a · auto-rollback |
jr_broker_use: Don't call SwarmJelly directly. Call it through SwarmFixer. The fixer enforces the 5-task contract, the TEMP setting, the taxonomy tagging, and the validator chain. A direct SwarmJelly call bypasses all of that and the receipt won't anchor. If you have a one-off research need, use the `whale:11434/api/generate` endpoint with TEMP=0.05 and document it as research-only · not production · no deed.

provenance.issuer_address: Donovan Mackey · 30yr CRE broker · $8B closed · Jupiter FL

scoring_impact_raw:
- **assignment_success**: HIGH · SwarmJelly IS the repair engine · its quality caps the whole refinery
- **repair_lift**: DIRECT · the lift distribution is the model's report card
- **validator_confidence**: MEDIUM · validators trust SwarmJelly proportional to its 7-day lift distribution
- **risk_temperature**: MEDIUM · model regression is a real risk · canary discipline mitigates
- **probability_of_close**: MEDIUM · customers don't buy the model · they buy the refinery output
- **evidence_strength**: NEUTRAL · model doesn't fabricate evidence at TEMP=0.05
- **cost_to_mint**: LOW · 4B model · runs on commodity GPUs · ~$0.05 per inference
sr_broker_use: The Sr Hack monitors SwarmJelly health weekly · reads the inference-throughput chart · watches for latency drift · catches model regressions before they ship a bad refinery run. The Sr Hack also signs off on every model version bump (v1.2 → v1.3) · the upgrade requires a 24-hour canary cook against the 7-mode test suite · the canary must show ≥ the prior version's lift distribution before the production tag flips.
street_definition: "The Jelly model." That's what the Hacks call it at standup. **SwarmJelly** is the in-house cooked 4-billion-parameter model that powers SwarmFixer. It's not the offense brain · it's not the customer's agent · it's the **defense model** that knows how to repair other models' work. Trained in-house. Open weights. Deployed at `whale:11434`. TEMP locked at 0.05.
tribunal_use:
- **Rule layer**: All SwarmFixer outputs MUST be generated by a SwarmJelly version on the approved-tag list (C02 model-config compliance)
- **Rule layer**: Inferences not at TEMP=0.05 are rejected at receipt anchoring (C02)
- **Judge layer**: The Tribunal doesn't grade SwarmJelly directly · it grades the REPAIR task output · SwarmJelly's quality is measured by aggregate Repair Lift over a 7-day window
- **Classification impact**: a SwarmJelly version flagged with > 20% PROPOLIS rate over 7 days gets rolled back to the prior tag · auto-rollback
